description: Sharing a multi-byte variable or buffer between an ISR and a task without volatile or a critical section, causing torn reads and lost updates.
body: Failure mode: an ISR writes a 32-bit value or a buffer that a task reads without synchronization on an 8/16-bit core where the access is not atomic; the task occasionally reads a half-updated value, or a missing volatile lets the compiler cache a stale copy. Detection signals: rare, non-reproducible corruption tied to interrupt timing; bug vanishes when interrupts are slowed or optimization is lowered; works in debug, fails in release. Do not share ISR/task data without protection. Safer fallback: mark shared variables volatile, guard multi-byte access with a short critical section or a lock-free ring buffer, keep the protected region minimal, and stress the interrupt to confirm no torn reads.
